meta_description: Researchers introduce AGORA, an inference-free step-level compressor combining a structural prompt parser and a learned scorer to improve LLM performance.
body_html:
AGORA is the only inference-free method retaining >= 75% uncompressed performance in 8 of 9 cells.

Step-Granularity CompressionAction-Grammar DestructionToken-Level Extractive Compressors Fail Cell IDMean RewardRealized Compression17 (env, backbone, method)mean reward <= 0.051.3-13.3x realized compression 125M-parameter Relevance Scorer Trained on Counterfactual Next-Action-Change Labels (~2ms/step, zero per-step LLM toll) A Four-Way Component Ablation Isolates the Structural Floor as the Dominant Quality LeverThe Learned Scorer is the Source of 1.0-11.5x Adaptive End-to-End Compression from a Single Fixed Keep Ratio Key Takeaway: AGORA Outperforms Inference-Free and LLM-Based Methods in Compressing LLM Agents

source.title: AGORA: Adapter-Grounded Observation-Action Retention for Inference-Free Prompt Compression in LLM Agents
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called AGORA to compress language models (LLMs) without sacrificing performance, known as inference-free prompt compression. The existing token-level extractive compressors are not suitable for LLM agents and fail to retain critical action semantics, leading to poor performance. AGORA addresses this issue by using a step-level compressor that combines a structural parser, a floor for format- and recency-critical content, and a learned relevance scorer. This approach has shown promising results, retaining over 75% of uncompressed performance in most cells tested.
